description: Service Worker for offline caching and Web Vitals reporting. Items 14 and 15.
steps: Create public/sw.js - a service worker that caches the app shell (HTML, CSS, JS bundles, fonts). Uses a cache-first strategy for static assets and network-first for API calls. Cache name should be versioned (e.g., 'music-app-v1'). On install, pre-cache the root page and key static assets. On fetch, serve from cache for same-origin requests, fall back to network.

Create app/components/ServiceWorkerRegistration.tsx - a Client Component that registers the service worker on mount. Uses navigator.serviceWorker.register('/sw.js') with proper error handling. Only registers in production (check process.env.NODE_ENV or window.location.hostname !== 'localhost').

Create lib/web-vitals.ts - a module that uses the web-vitals library pattern (manual implementation since we don't want to add a dependency). Uses PerformanceObserver to capture LCP, FID, CLS, FCP, TTFB metrics. Stores results in localStorage for now under key 'music-web-vitals'. Export a reportWebVitals function.
Create app/components/WebVitalsReporter.tsx - a Client Component that calls reportWebVitals on mount to start collecting metrics.
acceptance_criteria: public/sw.js exists and implements cache-first for static assets
Service worker is registered only in production environments
Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, FCP, TTFB) are collected and stored in localStorage
The OfflineDetector component still works correctly alongside the service worker
Build passes with zero TypeScript errors and zero lint errors
findings: Removed @ts-nocheck from sw.js since ESLint flagged it. Simplified error handling in ServiceWorkerRegistration to avoid unused console.warn eslint issue. Build produces NotImplementedError warnings from Bun worker_threads but build completes successfully.
